summary: Robbery complaints rose gradually from FY22 through FY24 before falling 10 percent in FY25, which NYPD attributed broadly to deterrence, intelligence and enforcement efforts across major felony categories. The much lower FY26 figure in this dataset covers only part of the fiscal year rather than a full 12 months, so it understates what a full-year total would look like and isn't directly comparable to earlier full-year figures.

sources.label: NYPD, Preliminary Fiscal 2026 Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: increasing overall; the FY26 figure is partial-year and understates the full total
